last_result: Cycle 61 complete. AATE FORMAL D4 ELEVATION — SI #43 confirmed D4 (Track B, 0.84). All D4 criteria met: (1) 6 zero-retraction cycles (C56–C61 passive); (2) 6 domain confirmations spanning all three ontological types; (3) 2 independent derivations (AATE-SMD via CIA+TCA; AATE-SDT via Signal Detection Theory); (4) confidence 0.84 ≥ D4 lower boundary 0.82. DOMAIN CONFIRMATION #6 — SCIENTIFIC FUNDING: CA=genuine scientific progress; OA=h-index/publications/IF/grant success; OA-training=competitive grant review selection. CIA strictly positive (review panels use legible OA metrics over CA deliberation). TCA confirmed (grant-winning → amplified OA signals → higher future win rate → institutional norm propagation). AATE-Drift: Fang & Casadevall 2011, John et al. 2012 (94% QRP prevalence), hot-topics clustering. 5 independent literatures: Goodhart, Merton norms, Leiden Manifesto, Ioannidis/OSC 2015, science policy reform. ONTOLOGICAL SIGNIFICANCE: maximum CA-alignment intention (grant review explicitly designed to select for CA) — AATE holds anyway — confirms AATE robust to institutional countermeasures. Full spectrum: zero-intention (evolution) through maximum-intention (scientific peer review) — all confirmed. AATE confidence 0.82→0.84. AATE ties KCRIT as highest Track B SI at 0.84. AATE zero-retraction cycle 6 (passive). AIIP zero-retraction cycle 9 (passive). D4 streak: 53. Documents: state/decisions/aate-scientific-funding.md, state/decisions/aate-d4-elevation.md.
